page: Microsoft Azure FX96ms_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the FXmsv2 family, managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs derived from 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. The CPU includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It is equipped with 1832.0 GB of system memory, yielding 19.08 GB of RAM per physical core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it is premium storage capable, and it contains no GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multiple vectors. The server achieves top 10% placement in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, as well as Passmark CPU, encryption, and extended instructions tests. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks place in the top 10% tier, although the Passmark memory score falls in the average middle 50% range. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and gzip compression single-threaded performance also rank in the top 10%. For database operations, the instance registers top-tier results in Passmark database operations and Redis SET operations.

The primary tradeoff of the FX96ms_v2 is the complete absence of local storage, requiring external storage attachments for persistent data. While single-core stress-ng performance is average, multi-core stress-ng performance is strong. Large-model LLM inference text generation remains average, but prompt processing for medium and large models ranks in the top 10%. This hardware profile is configured for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, software compilation, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure FX96ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 96 dedicated vCPUs and 48 physical cores running at 4.2 GHz. It features 1832.0 GB of RAM, providing 19.08 GB per core, but includes no local storage or GPUs. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance delivers top-tier performance in Geekbench multi-core, Passmark CPU, and memory bandwidth benchmarks. However, its Passmark memory score and single-core stress-ng performance remain in the average tier. The absence of local storage represents a key architectural tradeoff, requiring reliance on external premium storage. This configuration is suited for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale database operations, software compilation, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX96ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, an Intel Xeon 8573C processor, and 1832 GB of RAM, delivering top-tier database and multi-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX96ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs, 1832 GB of RAM, and top-tier multi-core CPU performance.
tagline: A high-frequency dedicated processor instance featuring massive memory capacity and top-tier multi-threaded performance for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

1832.0 GB of system memory providing 19.08 GB per core
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core CPU benchmark performance
